question: List all actors sorted by last name.
sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM actor ORDER BY last_name LIMIT 10

question: How many films are in the database?
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_films FROM film

question: What are the five most expensive films to rent?
sql: SELECT title, rental_rate FROM film ORDER BY rental_rate DESC LIMIT 5

question: What are the ten longest films?
sql: SELECT title, length FROM film ORDER BY length DESC LIMIT 10

question: What is the average rental rate across all films?
sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(rental_rate)::numeric, 2) AS avg_rental_rate FROM film

question: How many films are there per rating?
sql: SELECT rating, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM film GROUP BY rating ORDER BY cnt DESC
notes: Covers all MPAA ratings: G, PG, PG-13, R, NC-17.

question: List active customers sorted by last name.
sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, email FROM customer WHERE activebool = TRUE ORDER BY last_name LIMIT 5

question: Which films have a replacement cost above $25?
sql: SELECT title, replacement_cost FROM film WHERE replacement_cost > 25 ORDER BY replacement_cost DESC LIMIT 5

question: List PG-13 rated films with their descriptions.
sql: SELECT title, description FROM film WHERE rating = 'PG-13' ORDER BY title LIMIT 5
notes: String filter on rating enum column.

question: What film categories are available?
sql: SELECT name FROM category ORDER BY name

question: Which films have a rental duration of 3 days or less?
sql: SELECT title FROM film WHERE rental_duration <= 3 ORDER BY title LIMIT 10

question: Who are the active staff members?
sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM staff WHERE active = TRUE
notes: active is a boolean column on staff.

question: How many active customers does the store have?
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_customers FROM customer WHERE activebool = TRUE

question: List all films released in 2006.
sql: SELECT title, release_year FROM film WHERE release_year = 2006 ORDER BY title LIMIT 10

question: What are the distinct film ratings used in the catalog?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT rating FROM film ORDER BY rating

question: Which actors have appeared in the most films?
sql: SELECT a.first_name, a.last_name, COUNT(fa.film_id) AS film_count FROM actor a JOIN film_actor fa ON a.actor_id = fa.actor_id GROUP BY a.actor_id, a.first_name, a.last_name ORDER BY film_count DESC LIMIT 10
notes: Many-to-many join via film_actor bridge table.

question: What are the ten most frequently rented films?
sql: SELECT f.title, COUNT(r.rental_id) AS rental_count FROM film f JOIN inventory i ON f.film_id = i.film_id JOIN rental r ON i.inventory_id = r.inventory_id GROUP BY f.film_id, f.title ORDER BY rental_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: Which customers have spent more than $150 in total?
sql: SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(p.amount) AS total_spent FROM customer c JOIN payment p ON c.customer_id = p.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name HAVING SUM(p.amount) > 150 ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 10

question: List all Action films sorted alphabetically.
sql: SELECT f.title, cat.name AS category FROM film f JOIN film_category fc ON f.film_id = fc.film_id JOIN category cat ON fc.category_id = cat.category_id WHERE cat.name = 'Action' ORDER BY f.title LIMIT 10

question: What is the total revenue generated by each store?
sql: SELECT s.store_id, SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue FROM store s JOIN staff st ON s.store_id = st.store_id JOIN payment p ON st.staff_id = p.staff_id GROUP BY s.store_id
notes: Revenue attributed via staff who processed payments.

question: Show the most expensive films to rent along with their language.
sql: SELECT f.title, f.rental_rate, l.name AS language FROM film f JOIN language l ON f.language_id = l.language_id ORDER BY f.rental_rate DESC LIMIT 10
notes: Simple FK join to resolve language name.

question: Who are the top 10 customers by number of rentals?
sql: SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, COUNT(r.rental_id) AS rental_count FROM customer c JOIN rental r ON c.customer_id = r.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name ORDER BY rental_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: How many films are in each category?
sql: SELECT cat.name, COUNT(fc.film_id) AS film_count FROM category cat JOIN film_category fc ON cat.category_id = fc.category_id GROUP BY cat.name ORDER BY film_count DESC

question: Find Drama films longer than 2 hours.
sql: SELECT f.title, f.length FROM film f JOIN film_category fc ON f.film_id = fc.film_id JOIN category c ON fc.category_id = c.category_id WHERE f.length > 120 AND c.name = 'Drama' ORDER BY f.length DESC LIMIT 10
notes: Composite filter on length and category.

question: Which customers are from the United States?
sql: SELECT cu.first_name, cu.last_name, co.country FROM customer cu JOIN address a ON cu.address_id = a.address_id JOIN city ci ON a.city_id = ci.city_id JOIN country co ON ci.country_id = co.country_id WHERE co.country = 'United States' ORDER BY cu.last_name LIMIT 10
notes: Four-table join traversing the address hierarchy.

question: What is the average payment amount per film rating?
sql: SELECT f.rating, ROUND(AVG(p.amount)::numeric, 2) AS avg_payment FROM film f JOIN inventory i ON f.film_id = i.film_id JOIN rental r ON i.inventory_id = r.inventory_id JOIN payment p ON r.rental_id = p.rental_id GROUP BY f.rating ORDER BY avg_payment DESC
notes: Full chain join from film to payment.

question: Which films have the most copies in inventory?
sql: SELECT f.title, COUNT(i.inventory_id) AS copies_in_stock FROM film f JOIN inventory i ON f.film_id = i.film_id GROUP BY f.film_id, f.title ORDER BY copies_in_stock DESC LIMIT 10

question: What is the total revenue per month?
sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', payment_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS monthly_revenue FROM payment GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

question: Show cheap Comedy films with a rental rate below $3.
sql: SELECT f.title, f.rental_rate FROM film f JOIN film_category fc ON f.film_id = fc.film_id JOIN category c ON fc.category_id = c.category_id WHERE c.name = 'Comedy' AND f.rental_rate < 3.00 ORDER BY f.rental_rate, f.title LIMIT 10
notes: Compound filter on category and price.

question: Who are the top 10 customers by total amount paid and how many times did they rent?
sql: SELECT cu.first_name, cu.last_name, COUNT(r.rental_id) AS rentals, SUM(p.amount) AS total_paid FROM customer cu JOIN rental r ON cu.customer_id = r.customer_id JOIN payment p ON r.rental_id = p.rental_id GROUP BY cu.customer_id, cu.first_name, cu.last_name ORDER BY total_paid DESC LIMIT 10

question: Which films have never been rented?
sql: SELECT f.title FROM film f WHERE f.film_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT i.film_id FROM inventory i JOIN rental r ON i.inventory_id = r.inventory_id) ORDER BY f.title LIMIT 10
notes: Anti-join pattern using NOT IN with correlated subquery.

question: Which customers have spent above the average customer spend?
sql: WITH customer_spend AS (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) AS total_spent FROM payment GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, cs.total_spent FROM customer c JOIN customer_spend cs ON c.customer_id = cs.customer_id WHERE cs.total_spent > (SELECT AVG(total_spent) FROM customer_spend) ORDER BY cs.total_spent DESC LIMIT 10
notes: CTE with scalar subquery for dynamic threshold.

question: How many films has each actor appeared in, broken down by category?
sql: SELECT a.first_name, a.last_name, cat.name AS category, COUNT(fa.film_id) AS films_in_category FROM actor a JOIN film_actor fa ON a.actor_id = fa.actor_id JOIN film_category fc ON fa.film_id = fc.film_id JOIN category cat ON fc.category_id = cat.category_id GROUP BY a.actor_id, a.first_name, a.last_name, cat.category_id, cat.name ORDER BY films_in_category DESC LIMIT 10

question: Which customers have never rented an Action film?
sql: SELECT cu.first_name, cu.last_name FROM customer cu WHERE cu.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT r.customer_id FROM rental r JOIN inventory i ON r.inventory_id = i.inventory_id JOIN film_category fc ON i.film_id = fc.film_id JOIN category c ON fc.category_id = c.category_id WHERE c.name = 'Action') ORDER BY cu.last_name LIMIT 10
notes: Deep anti-join across the full rental chain.

question: Rank months by revenue within each year.
sql: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM payment_date) AS year, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM payment_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS revenue, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM payment_date) ORDER BY SUM(amount) DESC) AS rank_in_year FROM payment GROUP BY year, month ORDER BY year, rank_in_year LIMIT 24
notes: Window function RANK() partitioned by year over monthly aggregates.

question: Who are the top 5 customers by rental count using window ranking?
sql: WITH ranked AS (SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, COUNT(r.rental_id) AS rental_count, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(r.rental_id) DESC) AS rnk FROM customer c JOIN rental r ON c.customer_id = r.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name) SELECT first_name, last_name, rental_count, rnk FROM ranked WHERE rnk <= 5
notes: CTE wrapping a window function to filter by rank.

question: What are the top 10 films by total revenue, including rental count and average payment?
sql: SELECT f.title, COUNT(r.rental_id) AS times_rented, ROUND(SUM(p.amount)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue, ROUND(AVG(p.amount)::numeric, 2) AS avg_payment FROM film f JOIN inventory i ON f.film_id = i.film_id JOIN rental r ON i.inventory_id = r.inventory_id JOIN payment p ON r.rental_id = p.rental_id GROUP BY f.film_id, f.title ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10
notes: Full chain aggregation with multiple metrics.

question: Which countries generate the most revenue and how many customers do they have?
sql: SELECT co.country, COUNT(DISTINCT cu.customer_id) AS customers, ROUND(SUM(p.amount)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM country co JOIN city ci ON co.country_id = ci.country_id JOIN address a ON ci.city_id = a.city_id JOIN customer cu ON a.address_id = cu.address_id JOIN payment p ON cu.customer_id = p.customer_id GROUP BY co.country_id, co.country ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10
notes: Five-table join with COUNT DISTINCT for deduplicated customer count.

question: For each film, how does its rental rate compare to the average rental rate of its category?
sql: SELECT f.title, f.rental_rate, AVG(f.rental_rate) OVER (PARTITION BY fc.category_id) AS avg_rate_in_category, f.rental_rate - AVG(f.rental_rate) OVER (PARTITION BY fc.category_id) AS diff_from_category_avg FROM film f JOIN film_category fc ON f.film_id = fc.film_id ORDER BY diff_from_category_avg DESC LIMIT 10
notes: Window AVG partitioned by category without collapsing rows.

question: Show month-over-month revenue growth rate.
sql: WITH monthly AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', payment_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS revenue FROM payment GROUP BY month) SELECT month, revenue, LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_revenue, ROUND((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / NULLIF(LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month), 0) * 100, 2) AS pct_change FROM monthly ORDER BY month
notes: CTE + LAG window function for MoM percentage change. NULLIF guards against division by zero.
